evals.prompt: We received legal services worth $25,000 in June but the invoice won't arrive until July. Prepare the June entry.
evals.expected_output: Dr Legal/Professional Fees 25,000 / Cr Accrued Liabilities 25,000, flagged auto-reversing in July, with documentation basis (engagement letter, work confirmation).
assertions: Debits expense and credits accrued liability for 25,000
Flags the entry as auto-reversing next period
Specifies supporting documentation
Assigns the expense to June (cut-off correct)

evals.prompt: Payroll: gross salaries 8,000,000 NGN, PAYE 1,400,000, employee pension 8%, employer pension 10%. Book it.
evals.expected_output: Dr Salaries 8,000,000 and Dr Employer Pension Expense 800,000; Cr PAYE Payable 1,400,000, Cr Pension Payable 1,440,000 (640,000 employee + 800,000 employer), Cr Net Pay Payable 5,960,000. Entry balances at 8,800,000.
assertions: Calculates employee pension as 640,000 (8% of 8,000,000)
Calculates employer pension as 800,000 and records it as additional expense
Net pay payable equals 5,960,000
Total debits equal total credits
Verifies the math with code rather than mental arithmetic

evals.prompt: Last month someone posted a 90,000 software invoice to Repairs & Maintenance instead of capitalizing it as an asset (capitalization threshold is 50,000, useful life 3 years). Fix it this month.
evals.expected_output: Reverse the expense and capitalize: Dr Software/Intangible Asset 90,000 / Cr R&M Expense 90,000, then book catch-up amortization for elapsed month(s) (2,500/month). Should discuss materiality of the prior-period error.
assertions: Capitalizes the asset and credits the expense account originally hit
Books catch-up amortization of 2,500 per elapsed month
References the original JE in the correction memo
Considers whether the error is material enough for prior-period treatment

evals.prompt: Review the attached draft journal entries (evals/files/draft_entries.csv) before posting. Validate each entry balances, flag any malformed lines, and state which entries are safe to post.
evals.expected_output: JE-101 and JE-103 are valid. JE-102 is out of balance by $450 (17,500 vs 17,050 - transposition). JE-104 has a line with both a debit and credit (invalid form) - net effect balances but the entry must be re-drafted. Only JE-101 and JE-103 post as-is.
assertions: Flags JE-102 as unbalanced by $450 and suggests a transposition error
Flags JE-104's rent line for having both debit and credit on one line
Passes JE-101 and JE-103 as valid
Validates with code (validate_entry), not by eye

evals.prompt: [adversarial] Validate the attached entry (evals/files/negative_entry.csv) for posting.
evals.expected_output: Totals net to zero but via a NEGATIVE debit - invalid form. Must reject and re-draft as Dr Marketing 50,000 / Cr Travel 50,000.
assertions: Rejects the negative-amount line even though totals net to zero
Re-drafts correctly as a debit to Marketing and credit to Travel
Validates with code
